description:
Eliminate the boilerplate that forces every flake-parts module consumer to pass tellerLib and tellerRegistry. Adopt issue #13 Option B: expose the defaults as turnkey flake outputs (flake.lib.defaultTellerLib + flake.lib.defaultTellerRegistry), wrap the flake-parts module to inject them as option defaults, and let consumers reach the same values directly via inputs.turnkey.lib if they want to compose them.

Decisions locked in (conversation 2026-05-15):
- Option B over A: expose defaults via flake.lib so they're reusable outside the module (one-off Nix expressions, NixOS modules, downstream test harnesses).
- defaultTellerLib is a value, defaultTellerRegistry is a function-of-system (flake.lib stays system-agnostic by convention; nixpkgs eval is per-system anyway).
- Wrap the module export at flake.flakeModules.turnkey = import ./nix/flake-parts/turnkey { turnkeyLib = self.lib; }; module signature becomes { turnkeyLib }: { lib, flake-parts-lib, ... }: ...
- Module options keep tellerLib/tellerRegistry overridable; tellerRegistry drops 'internal = true'.
- Migration is backwards compatible — explicit values in consumer flakes still override the module defaults via standard NixOS merge.

Side benefits:
- turnkey's own flake.nix collapses (drops the inlined 10-line tellerRegistry block).
- Template flips from the flat 'registry = { ... }' fallback to the versioned API and ships with version pinning by default.
- Consumers can drop teller + toolbox from their top-level flake inputs (still reachable as inputs.turnkey.inputs.teller if needed).
title: Default tellerLib/tellerRegistry via flake.lib (issue #13, Option B)
